violations.text: The spray hose at the dishwasher was not in good repair, covered in tape. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2025
The oil recycling bin outside has an accumulation of grease and debris. Receptacles must be maintained clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/07/2025

Open employees' beverage containers were observed stored on the clean side of dishwasher with dishes. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

Chicken and shrimp in the prep unit observed held at 44-46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chicken and shrimp was placed in the freezer to rapidly chill. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Soup was placed hot in covered plastic buckets greater than four inches deep, not in refrigeration. Cooling logs must be maintained for inspector to review at Reinspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

Chicken observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler. 4 buckets of soup observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food observed on the floor in the kitchen, walk in freezer, and on shelves less than 6 inches above the floor. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (against liquor bottle in speed rail) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
Ice bucket was stored on the floor of the bar area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
Blender bowl observed covered in tape, which is not smooth and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the dry ingredient bins, ice bucket, and can opener base have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. The portable fan in the sushi area has an accumulation of dust and debris. NonFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

The grease recycling bin outside is located on gravel. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Establishment has a lack of managerial control, and a food safety consultant is required. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Spanish Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Chinese Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Employee observed filling a container with water in the kitchen hand-washing sink, beside the food prep sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

An unwrapped pig head was observed directly on the floor in the walk in freezer. Open food containers observed on storage shelf in kitchen, and in refrigerator. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or wrapping. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as tomatoes) in the prep unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Chicken, shrimp, and cut lettuce observed held at 46-48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Chicken observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Working containers of numerous ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Food observed stored on the floor in the kitchen, walk in cooler, and walk in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Bowls and containers are being used as scoops in containers of numerous dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Water observed in numerous stacked food containers.Equipment and utensils must be air dried after cleaning and sanitizing.An air drying rack is needed in the kitchen. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the front service area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
Single-use items (old food containers) are being reused for food preparation and storage.Single-use items shall not be reused. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
The large trash bin used for rice storage, and plastic containers used as scoops were not in good repair, cracked and/or with pieces missing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
The mop sink was not in good repair, nonfunctional at time of inspection.One leg is missing, and containers are used to hold it up. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

Prep unit were observed in poor repair in the kitchen, with food in the top not held at 41 degrees or colder. Food requiring refrigeration may be held in the lower portion only. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigerator have an accumulation of soil residue, mold, and other debris.Shelf in the front cooking area, and food storage bins have an accumulation of debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
Mop water is not disposed of in the service sink, as service sink was nonfunctional. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025
The door to one toilet room in the dining area is not self-closing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025

The grease recycling bin outside is located on gravel. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025
Ceiling light covers have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Chinese Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Employees did not wash hands before beginning to work with food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by carts. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Opened containers of food observed stacked with top containers in direct contact with food below. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or wrapping. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer, knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. All items are to be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Shrimp, lettuce, and lettuce salads observed held at degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Discussed not stacking containers in the top portion of the prep unit. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Pork, soup, and shrimp was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Working containers of ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Food observed stored on the floor in the kitchen, sushi area, walk in cooler, and walk in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Plasticware displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Single-use items (old food containers) are being used for food preparation and storage.Single-use items shall not be reused. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the kitchen and front cooking area. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Aluminum wrap is used to line shelves in the kitchen and front cooking area.Plastic wrap observed tied around several shelves in areas of the kitchen. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
The doors on the ice machine and kitchen prep unit are not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:curtain between kitchen and dining roomprep units including door handlesrefrigerators including door trackswalk in cooler door including door handleshelvesbin for plastic utensilsbin for spices in cooking area6 burner stovedry ingredient bins New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025
Mop water is dumped outside per PIC.The mop sink must be used to dispose of mop water.Mop sink was observed to be completely hidden by an accumulation of items. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
The door to one toilet room in the dining area is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025

The grease recycling receptacle outside is located on gravel/dirt. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025
